content: China's macro backdrop continues to show slowing growth with policy easing under way. Recent PBOC rate cuts have supported a modest reflationary impulse but property-sector deleveraging remains a meaningful headwind. Q1 real GDP printed 4.7% YoY, below trend. Manufacturing PMI ticked back into expansion at 50.4 but services PMI softened to 49.6 — mixed risk appetite signals across the activity complex. Valuation backdrop is constructive — CSI 300 trades near 11.5x forward, well below 10-year average. Currency stability supports near-term sentiment but flows remain cautious. Note: this report does not carry an explicit entropy reading.
